   - Therefore, `sSup (B N hN) ≤ sSup (⋃ N', ⋃ (hN' : N' ∈ D), B N' hN')`, because the supremum of a subset is less than or equal to the supremum of the set.

Thus, `N ≤ sSup (⋃ N', ⋃ (hN' : N' ∈ D), B N' hN')`.

have h₁ : sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) = sSup D := by
      calc
        sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) = sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) := rfl
        _ = sSup (⋃ N : {N : PersistenceSubmodule M // N ∈ D}, (B N.val N.property)) := by
          simp [Set.ext_iff]
          <;>
          (try aesop) <;>
          (try
            {
              simp_all [Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
              <;>
              aesop
            })
          <;>
          (try
            {
              aesop
            })
        _ = sSup (⋃ N : {N : PersistenceSubmodule M // N ∈ D}, (B N.val N.property)) := rfl
        _ = sSup D := by
          calc
            sSup (⋃ N : {N : PersistenceSubmodule M // N ∈ D}, (B N.val N.property)) =
                sSup (⋃ N : {N : PersistenceSubmodule M // N ∈ D}, (B N.val N.property)) := rfl
            _ = sSup (Set.range fun (N : {N : PersistenceSubmodule M // N ∈ D}) => sSup (B N.val N.property)) := by
              simp [Set.ext_iff, sSup_iUnion]
              <;>
              (try aesop) <;>
              (try
                {
                  simp_all [Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
                  <;>
                  aesop
                })
              <;>
              (try
                {
                  aesop
                })
            _ = sSup (Set.range fun (N : {N : PersistenceSubmodule M // N ∈ D}) => (N.val : PersistenceSubmodule M)) := by
              congr 1
              ext x
              simp only [Set.mem_range]
              constructor
              · rintro ⟨N, rfl⟩
                use N
                rw [hB]
              · rintro ⟨N, rfl⟩
                use ⟨N, by simp_all [Set.mem_range]⟩
                <;> simp_all [hB]
                <;> aesop
            _ = sSup D := by
              calc
                sSup (Set.range fun (N : {N : PersistenceSubmodule M // N ∈ D}) => (N.val : PersistenceSubmodule M)) =
                    sSup (Set.range fun (N : {N : PersistenceSubmodule M // N ∈ D}) => (N.val : PersistenceSubmodule M)) := rfl
                _ = sSup (Set.range (fun (N : {N : PersistenceSubmodule M // N ∈ D}) => (N.val : PersistenceSubmodule M))) := rfl
                _ = sSup D := by
                  have h₂ : Set.range (fun (N : {N : PersistenceSubmodule M // N ∈ D}) => (N.val : PersistenceSubmodule M)) = D := by
                    ext x
                    simp [Set.mem_range, Set.mem_coe]
                    <;>
                    (try aesop)
                    <;>
                    (try
                      {
                        aesop
                      })
                  rw [h₂]
                  <;>
                  (try aesop)
                  <;>
                  (try
                    {
                      aesop
                    })
    have h₂ : sSup D = ⊤ := D.sSup_eq_top'
    rw [h₁]
    rw [h₂]

        -- Show that the supremum of the union is the same as the supremum of D
        have h₁ : (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) ⊆ D := by
          intro x hx
          -- Simplify the union membership to get an element N in D and a proof x ∈ B N hN
          simp only [Set.mem_iUnion, exists_prop] at hx
          rcases hx with ⟨N, rfl, hx'⟩
          exact ⟨N, hx'⟩
        have h₂ : sSup D ≤ sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) := by
          -- Use the fact that the union is a subset of D to get sSup D ≤ sSup (union)
          apply sSup_le_sSup h₁
          <;> simp_all [D.sSup_eq_top]
          <;> aesop
        have h₃ : sSup (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) ≤ sSup D := by
          -- Use the fact that each N in D is the supremum of B N hN to get sSup (union) ≤ sSup D
          have h₄ : ∀ (N : PersistenceSubmodule M) (hN : N ∈ D), sSup (B N hN) ≤ sSup D := by
            intro N hN
            have h₅ : sSup (B N hN) = N := by
              rw [hB N hN]
            rw [h₅]
            apply le_sSup
            exact ⟨N, hN⟩
          have h₅ : ∀ (x : ?m), x ∈ (⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN) → x ≤ sSup D := by
            intro x hx
            -- For each x in the union, x ≤ sSup D
            have h₆ : ∃ (N : ?m), ∃ (hN : N ∈ D), x ∈ B N hN := by
              simp only [Set.mem_iUnion, exists_prop] at hx
              exact hx
            rcases h₆ with ⟨N, hN, hx'⟩
            have h₇ : x ≤ sSup (B N hN) := by
              apply le_sSup
              exact ⟨x, hx'⟩
            have h₈ : sSup (B N hN) ≤ sSup D := h₄ N hN
            exact le_trans h₇ h₈
          exact sSup_le h₅
        -- Combine h₂ and h₃ to get the equality
        exact le_antisymm h₃ h₂
      _ = ⊤ := by
        -- The supremum of D is ⊤ by D.sSup_eq_top'
        rw [D.sSup_eq_top']
        <;> aesop

  -- The following line is the one that needs to be adjusted as per the modified proof strategy.
  -- Instead of using `iSup_coe`, we can directly use the fact that the pointwise iSup is the top.
  -- This change avoids the need for `iSup_coe` and relies on `h₂` to conclude the proof.
  have h₆ : ⨆ (p : PersistenceSubmodule M) (hp : p ∈ D), p x = ⊤ := by

instead think of it as `⋃ s ∈ D.carrier, (⋃ (hN : s ∈ D), B s hN) = ⋃ s ∈ D.carrier, (B s ‹s ∈ D›)` because for each `s ∈ D.carrier`, there is only one proof `s ∈ D`.

So `⋃ N, ⋃ (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN = ⋃ (N : _) (hN : N ∈ D), B N hN = ⋃ s ∈ D.carrier, B s ‹s ∈ D›`.

But it doesn't matter for our purposes
